blocks.content: Her face on the wall, looking out the way she had always looked out — with openness, with willingness, with the expression of someone who had decided that whatever was coming was worth meeting.
He sat with his hands between his knees, the way he always sat when he was thinking, and after a long time he said:

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I miss you so much, Cass.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You were the best thing that's ever happened to me. I hope you always knew that. I hope I said it enough.
He looked at her face on the wall.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I don't know what I'm going to do without you. I'm figuring that out. I'm going to keep figuring it out.

The fountain behind him. The park around him. People passing on the path — some of them slowing to look at the mural, some of them stopping, some of them moving on without knowing what they were walking past.
She would have liked that. People not knowing. Just feeling something and not being sure why.
His voice broke on the last word.
